rationale: Round-1 hard_block ADDRESSED. BENCH-MOVED.md's two links are repaired to .mindspec/adr/ADR-0027-mindspec-otel-only.md and .mindspec/adr/ADR-0028-bench-rescue-procedure.md; both targets exist on disk and are git-tracked, so they resolve. I re-ran completeness independently: a path-resolving link scanner over all 395 non-docs_archive tracked .md files found 0 links resolving under a dead tree (.mindspec/docs/, top-level docs/, root review/) and 0 dangling links of any kind. A broad textual grep confirms the residual `.mindspec/docs/` strings are prose/inline-code spans, not live `](...)` links, and there are zero reference-style or relative inline links into the dead trees. BENCH-MOVED.md was the sole casualty and is now fixed; nothing else remains broken. R6 classifier sanity (correctness): isMoverRunState uses slash-anchored prefixes .mindspec/lineage/ and .mindspec/migrations/ (run-state only — manifests/snapshots/plan.md), and isVestigialConfigDrop uses exact equality on .mindspec/policies.yml and .mindspec/docs/glossary.md. Neither over-matches governable source — config.yaml and .mindspec/{specs,adr,domains,core}/** stay governed (the test asserts isMoverRunState(config.yaml)==false and isVestigialConfigDrop(ADR-0018.md)==false). Validate package tests pass. No new defect; the round-1 move is intact.

findings.issue: BENCH-MOVED.md links [ADR-0027](.mindspec/adr/ADR-0027-mindspec-otel-only.md) and [ADR-0028](.mindspec/adr/ADR-0028-bench-rescue-procedure.md) now point to the flat .mindspec/adr/ targets; both targets exist on disk and are git-tracked, so both resolve. My round-1 hard_block is fully repaired.

Path-resolving scanner over 395 non-docs_archive tracked .md files: 0 links resolve under .mindspec/docs/, docs/, or review/, and 0 dangling links overall. Broad grep confirms remaining `.mindspec/docs/` occurrences are prose / inline-code spans (skills, ADR-0014/0017, ARCHITECTURE/CONVENTIONS, domain architecture, migration plan.md), not live markdown links. No reference-style or relative inline links into the dead trees. BENCH-MOVED.md was the only casualty; it is now fixed, so the moved tree is 0-dangling and no root/non-moved-tree doc link remains broken.

isMoverRunState matches only slash-anchored .mindspec/lineage/ and .mindspec/migrations/ (verified contents are run-state: manifest.json, classification/inventory/lineage/plan/state snapshots, plan.md, staging copies). isVestigialConfigDrop is exact equality on .mindspec/policies.yml and .mindspec/docs/glossary.md (glossary also caught by isDocFile's .mindspec/docs/ prefix — defense-in-depth). Real governable .mindspec/ source is untouched: .mindspec/config.yaml and .mindspec/{specs,adr,domains,core}/** are NOT matched by either new predicate, so they remain subject to the adr-divergence gate. The new test asserts both the positive classification and the precision negatives (isMoverRunState(config.yaml)==false, isVestigialConfigDrop(ADR-0018.md)==false). `go test ./internal/validate` passes.
